relevant_files.annotation: Entry point for user onboarding. Writes .fabric/ evidence pack, installs .claude/ protocol files, and emits the 'Reason:' stdout trigger. All user-facing stdout strings are hardcoded in English ('Created', 'Installed', 'Next:', 'Reason:', 'ABORT'). CLI meta description is in Chinese: '在目标项目中初始化 Fabric。'

Produces human-readable scan report via console.log. All output labels hardcoded in English ('Fabric scan report', 'Target:', 'Framework:', 'README quality:'). CLI meta description is in Chinese: '扫描项目以检测 Fabric 引导候选模块。'

Launches fab serve, prints 'Fabric Dashboard: http://...' to stdout. CLI description is mixed: Chinese prefix + English suffix: '启动 Fabric 本地 MCP HTTP 服务。set FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN to enable Bearer auth (required for non-localhost binding).' Also contains an English console.error warning with emoji.
Installs 6-client AI bootstrap prompt files. CLI meta description in Chinese: '为支持的 AI 客户端安装 Fabric 引导提示模板。' Sub-command description in Chinese: '将 Fabric 引导模板复制到各客户端的原生位置。'
Installs Husky pre-commit hooks. CLI description in Chinese: '管理 Fabric Git 钩子模板。' / '安装 Fabric Husky pre-commit 钩子模板。' Target arg description in Chinese. Represents Day 4 of user journey.
Syncs .fabric/agents.meta.json after every AGENTS.md edit. CLI meta description in Chinese: '从 AGENTS.md 文件同步 Fabric 元数据。' Part of pre-commit three-piece set forming the daily commit path.
Guards @HUMAN locked blocks on every commit. CLI meta description in Chinese: '验证锁定的人工编辑区块。'
Appends one intent record per commit. CLI meta description in Chinese: '向 Fabric 意图日志添加一条记录。' Completes the pre-commit three-piece set.
CLI root entry; fab name + __CLI_VERSION__ + subcommand registry via citty. No description string of its own — version injected at build time.
Orchestrates the pre-commit three-piece set (sync-meta, human-lint, ledger-append). Part of the daily commit step of the user journey.
Generates MCP config for 6 AI clients (JSON/TOML dual-track). Part of user onboarding Day 2 path.

Produces ForensicReport (framework detection, topology, entry_points). Hard-codes schema version '1.0'. Evidence layer for AI-assisted AGENTS.md init. Chinese references: uses 'lang' field but no i18n abstraction.

Root SPA component. Contains the only product brand string: lowercase 'fabric' in sidebar logo, plus 'v1.1' version badge. Nav labels are English: 'Rules Tree', 'Human Lock', 'Intent Timeline', 'History Replay', 'Doctor'. Connection status is all-caps English: 'CONNECTED'/'CONNECTING'. Zero zh strings.
HTML shell. Title is 'Fabric Dashboard' (English). lang='en'. No zh locale support. Confirms Dashboard language is 100% English.

Complete design token inventory: 7 surface colors (dark slate palette), 5 state color groups (locked/stale/drift/approved/pending), 2 source color groups (ai=indigo, human=teal), 2 font families (Space Mono + Inter), 16 font sizes, 16 spacing steps, 6 border radii, z-index scale, motion tokens, layout dimensions. No brand color (no accent shared with product name). Primary action color is green (#22c55e).

Full UI component stylesheet. Brand class: .brand uses font-family-mono, bold, with .brand-logo (18px square, border, letter 'F') and .brand-version. All CSS class names are English. Responsive breakpoints at 960px and 720px. No theming for dark/light switching. AI column = indigo, human column = teal — the color coding is the semantic brand.
Rules Tree view. Subtitle hardcoded in English: '.fabric/agents.meta.json · L0/L1/L2 hierarchy · hash drift aware'. All UI strings English. Consumes AgentsMeta from @fabric/shared.
Human Lock vault view. All strings English. Reads HumanLockEntry. Part of 'ritualistic write' UX: approve button is the only write action.
Dual-column AI vs Human timeline. All strings English. Annotate button enables the second write action.
Doctor diagnostics view. Subtitle: 'fab doctor surface · framework, entry points, revision drift, protected paths'. All English. v1.1 feature surface.
History replay view. aria-label: 'Historical fabric rules tree'. All English. v1.1 extension E3.

MCP tool 'fab_get_rules'. Description: 'MANDATORY: Call before modifying any file to retrieve Fabric rules for a target path.' 100% English. param description 'Revision hash from prior fab_get_rules response; enables stale detection'.
MCP tool 'fab_append_intent'. Description: 'MANDATORY: Call after a completed task to append an intent ledger entry for Fabric.' 100% English.
MCP tool 'fab_update_registry'. Description: 'MANDATORY: Call to add, remove, or update Fabric registry nodes instead of editing registry files directly.' 100% English.
REST endpoint GET /api/rules. Part of Dashboard data path. English only.
REST endpoint GET /api/ledger?source=ai|human&since=<ts>. Dashboard intent timeline data source.
SSE endpoint GET /events. Pushes 'meta-changed'|'ledger-appended'|'human-lock-changed'. Drives Dashboard real-time updates.
Unified REST error response schema. Error text in English. Part of MCP surface i18n.
REST endpoint for fab doctor diagnostics. v1.1 surface. English only.

Claude Code bootstrap prompt installed by fab bootstrap. Content is 100% Chinese: 5-line Fabric protocol instruction set starting with '本项目使用 Fabric Protocol 管理规则。' This is the primary AI contract surface visible to Claude Code AI.
Gemini CLI bootstrap prompt. Identical Chinese 5-line content as CLAUDE.md. Confirms zh is the AI instruction language for all 6 clients.
Cursor MDC bootstrap rule (alwaysApply: true). Chinese 5-line content identical to CLAUDE.md. alwaysApply ensures it is always loaded.
Roo Code bootstrap. Same Chinese 5-line content.
Windsurf bootstrap. Same Chinese 5-line content.
Codex CLI AGENTS.md header. Same Chinese 5-line content. All 6 bootstrap files are zh-only.

Fallback AGENTS.md scaffold written by fab init when AI skill is not available. 100% English. Contains comment: '<!-- This is the fallback template. If you have Claude Code, run the agents-md-init skill for a semantically richer AGENTS.md. -->' Template variable {frameworkKind} and {projectName}. Represents brand voice in project-facing documentation.
Framework-specific AGENTS.md variant for Cocos Creator. Language unknown until read — likely English matching the template base.
Framework-specific AGENTS.md variant for Next.js.
Framework-specific AGENTS.md variant for Vite projects.

Claude Code skill template installed by fab init into target project. Content is mixed: English YAML frontmatter (name, description, allowed-tools), then Chinese body ('必须先 Read', '执行流程', 'Phase 1 — 框架确认', 'Phase 2 — 不变式提取', 'Phase 3 — 构造与落地'). The precondition message is Chinese: '请先运行 fab init 生成证据包'. The AI interview questions are in Chinese. Key AI-facing content is zh.
Claude Code Stop hook installed to target project. Sentinel check script that blocks completion until init-context.json exists. Language of error/block messages needs verification.

Primary user-facing doc for 7-stage journey from pnpm install to daily commit. 100% English. Covers Stage 1 (install) through Stage 7 (daily dev), state machine diagram, compatibility matrix, and troubleshooting. The single most important journey document.
Short quickstart doc. 100% English. Covers fab init → fab hooks install → fab bootstrap install → fab config install. Tracks the install-to-connect user path.

Canonical v1.0/v1.1 milestone boundary document. Status line: 'v1.0 = 7-day MVP (in progress); v1.1 = Maintenance milestone (deferred)'. Lists 4 v1.1 features: drift-check, fab migrate, fab doctor, Copilot fallback. Does NOT yet include v1.1 Feature #5 (Dashboard/fab serve) despite ANL-fabric-mcp-dashboard recommending its addition. 100% English.

Project README. 100% English. Uses 'Fabric v2.0' as product name. No tagline. No 'Consensus Plane'. No zh. Quick Start section still has placeholder content ('Placeholder workflow'). Day 7 Validation section added but Quick Start not yet updated to reflect fab serve.

Internal design thinking document in Chinese. Contains the origin of the product concept. The brainstorm file (BS-universal-ai-docs-2026-04-18/ideas/fabric-protocol.md) uses the term '织物协议' (fabric protocol in Chinese) in the original concept framing. 思路.md itself does not use 'Consensus Plane' — the concept is described functionally but not labeled with that English tagline.
Origin brainstorm document. Uses '织物协议' (fabric protocol in zh), '织物' as product metaphor origin. Also introduces the '5 components' framing. No 'Consensus Plane' term found.

Active 15-task lite-plan for v1.1 Dashboard + Doc-Init. Contains 'summary' and 'design_decisions' fields with English content. Milestone boundary: describes completed v1.0 MVP + in-progress v1.1 Wave A/B/C tasks. Key feature gate information.

CLI package: name '@fabric/fabric-cli', version '0.1.4'. This is the npm registry name users will type when doing pnpm install. Note the scoped name uses author handle 'fenglimg', not a 'fabric' org scope.
Server package: name '@fabric/fabric-server', version '0.1.0'. Same author-scoped naming pattern.
Dashboard package: name '@fabric/dashboard' (private: true), version '0.0.0'. Uses '@fabric' org scope — naming inconsistency with CLI/server using '@fabric' scope.
Shared types package: name '@fabric/shared'. Uses '@fabric' org scope. Inconsistency with CLI/server '@fabric' scope.
Monorepo root: name 'fabric', version '0.0.0'. No npm publish. Root version not bumped. Contains workspace definition.

patterns: naming: 'fab' is the CLI binary name; 'Fabric' is the product proper noun; 'fabric' (lowercase) appears in sidebar logo; '@fabric/fabric-*' is the current npm scope for CLI+server; '@fabric/*' is used for shared+dashboard — package scope is split across two namespaces
naming: 'Consensus Plane' does NOT appear anywhere in the codebase, docs, or templates — it is absent as a product tagline in all surfaces scanned
naming: '织物' (Chinese for 'fabric') appears only in the brainstorm/origin document as the original concept label; it is not used in any CLI output, Dashboard UI, or doc
naming: 'Fabric Protocol' appears in templates/bootstrap (all 6 files) as '本项目使用 Fabric Protocol 管理规则' — this is the canonical phrase used when addressing AI agents
zh/en split: CLI command meta descriptions = 100% Chinese (8/8 commands); CLI stdout output at runtime = 100% English; CLI --help would show zh descriptions but English examples in docs
zh/en split: All 6 bootstrap prompt templates = 100% Chinese (the AI instruction contract layer)
zh/en split: agents-md-init SKILL.md = mixed (English frontmatter metadata, Chinese body/interview content)
zh/en split: Dashboard UI labels = 100% English (nav, subtitles, badges, connection status)
zh/en split: All docs (initialization.md, quickstart.md, roadmap.md, README.md, day* docs) = 100% English
zh/en split: MCP tool descriptions (fab_get_rules, fab_append_intent, fab_update_registry) = 100% English
zh/en split: 思路.md design doc = Chinese (internal planning artifact)
text externalization: zero i18n infrastructure — no locale files, no t() calls, no language config; all strings are hardcoded inline
version state: CLI v0.1.4, server v0.1.0, dashboard v0.0.0, shared v0.0.0 — packages are at different version levels; no coordinated version strategy
milestone: roadmap.md records v1.0 = 7-day MVP (in-progress), v1.1 = 4 maintenance features (deferred); Dashboard (v1.1 Feature #5) is NOT yet in roadmap.md despite being built
milestone: active lite-plan covers v1.1 Wave A/B/C (15 tasks) including Dashboard + Doc-Init extension; estimated 6-8 days
brand: sidebar uses lowercase 'fabric' with monospace font and a bordered 'F' lettermark — minimal, technical brand identity
brand: dark slate + indigo (AI) + teal (human) is the functional color system; no single brand accent color; semantic coloring IS the brand
brand: README Quick Start section still has placeholder content; product name appears without tagline
brand: serve description is mixed zh+en: '启动 Fabric 本地 MCP HTTP 服务。set FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN...' — code-switch mid-sentence is a consistency gap
questions_for_user: npm scope split: CLI+server use '@fabric/fabric-*' while shared+dashboard use '@fabric/*'. Is the intention to unify under '@fabric' as the org scope for all public packages?

tagline: 'Consensus Plane' does not appear anywhere in the codebase. Was it ever intended as a tagline, or is it a concept from an earlier brainstorm phase that was abandoned? Should depth agents investigate a replacement tagline?

zh/en strategy: bootstrap prompt templates (CLAUDE.md etc.) are 100% Chinese. MCP tool descriptions and docs are 100% English. Is this split intentional — zh for AI instruction, en for developer-facing content?

CLI meta descriptions: all 8 command descriptions are in Chinese (e.g. '在目标项目中初始化 Fabric。') but CLI stdout output at runtime is in English. Should CLI help text be English to match docs, or is zh for --help intentional?
serve command description is code-switched: '启动 Fabric 本地 MCP HTTP 服务。set FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN to enable Bearer auth...' — should this be unified to one language?
Dashboard language: currently 100% English. Is zh/bilingual support planned for the Dashboard UI? If the target user is zh-speaking, what is the desired language for nav labels and view subtitles?

roadmap.md gap: Dashboard (v1.1 Feature #5 per ANL-fabric-mcp-dashboard) is built but not listed in roadmap.md. Should the roadmap be updated to include Feature #5 and the active lite-plan's Wave A-C progress?

brand color: the 'F' lettermark in the Dashboard sidebar uses a monospace bordered box. Is there a designed logo asset intended for use in docs/README, or is the text-based lettermark the final brand mark?
README Quick Start: still has 'Placeholder workflow' content. Is this a known gap to fill before v1.0 release?
version coordination: packages are at different versions (CLI 0.1.4, server 0.1.0, dashboard 0.0.0). Is there a plan for unified versioning at v1.0 release?

_metadata.analysis_mode: quick-scan (bash structural scan only; no Gemini invoked for this breadth-only Layer 1 task)
